Headline: The Chinese Exclusion Act & The Invention of Photo ID
Text: The US government introduced "Certificates of Residence" to properly regulate immigration and verify the legal status of Chinese laborers within the country.

Headline: The Birth of the Modern Booklet Passport
Text: The Paris Conference on Passports and Customs Formalities standardized the international passport booklet to make post-WWI diplomacy and global commerce efficient and safe.

Headline: The Belgian Colonial Identity Card
Text: The Belgian colonial administration introduced a standardized identity card system during a census to streamline local administration and modernize civil registry tracking.

Headline: The Dawn of Automated Data Sorting: IBM Hollerith Cards
Text: The German Reich statistical office utilized state-of-the-art punch-card sorting machines—provided by IBM’s German subsidiary, Dehomag—to efficiently catalog population data for public planning and national census logistics.

Headline: The Biometric Precursor: The Apartheid "Dompas"
Text: The "Natives Abolition of Passes and Co-ordination of Documents Act" aimed to streamline and modernize domestic identity paperwork for South African citizens.
